content: Title says all - should we have a config that enables as much rust code as possible for when we're making e.g. clippy options changes?

I am not sure it makes sense as a Kconfig symbol (I guess you mean one that selects a ton of things?), since it would require maintaining it manually etc.

Instead, in the past we discussed to have automated targets like e.g. rustallmodconfig that would figure the right configs instead.

In any case, note that one cannot really ensure that "everything" is enabled, given the constraints that symbols have.

Yeah I recognize that there's no way to get perfect coverage. rustallmodconfig would be fine too. Should that be filed? https://github.com/Rust-for-Linux/linux/issues?q=is%3Aissue%20state%3Aopen%20rustallmodconfig
